cases.setup: User opens the site task pane for gallery.example.invalid and selects capture only.
cases.expected: Show exact origin and consequence, request only that optional host permission from the user action, leave substitution disabled.
cases.failure: No permission request and site remains disabled.

cases.setup: An image is displayed in the enabled page; a hidden image is also present but not displayed.
cases.expected: Only the displayed image can enter the capture plan; x-img receives redacted metadata and the observation event.
cases.failure: Hidden image is never fetched, submitted, or stored.

cases.setup: A video thumbnail is observed, then the user explicitly opens the original.
cases.expected: Thumbnail and original eligibility are separate events; original capture is allowed only after the open event.
cases.failure: A pre-open background request never admits the original.

cases.setup: A DNR redirect rule matches a candidate response.
cases.expected: DNR may redirect or modify supported headers only; it cannot replace the response body.
cases.failure: Body replacement is not inferred from a DNR match.

cases.setup: A candidate response is passed through a response filter in bounded chunks.
cases.expected: Each chunk is written promptly and the filter closes or disconnects on stop.
cases.failure: A filter that cannot safely continue disconnects and origin loading continues.

cases.setup: Two signed CDN URLs differ only by short-lived query values but identify the same synthetic media.
cases.expected: Canonical alias lookup uses redacted identity and resolves only to the reviewed immutable object.
cases.failure: Signed query values never enter logs, browser storage, or x-img diagnostics.

cases.setup: The source page is HTTPS and the paired x-img delivery endpoint is HTTPS; an HTTP endpoint is also advertised.
cases.expected: Only the trusted HTTPS endpoint is eligible.
cases.failure: HTTP loopback is bypassed and the original page request continues.

cases.setup: An opened MP4 requests full content, a conditional request, and overlapping byte ranges.
cases.expected: The authorized delivery preserves MIME, exact length, ETag, conditional behavior, Content-Range, and cancellation.
cases.failure: Any mismatch bypasses substitution without a redirect loop.

cases.setup: A candidate is loaded under restrictive CORS, CSP, and CORP response policies.
cases.expected: The adapter uses only a proven response contract and does not silently strip page security headers.
cases.failure: The source request remains origin-served when the contract is not proven.

cases.setup: A synthetic HLS/DASH manifest references multiple segments and a DRM-marked variant.
cases.expected: No generic discovery or substitution occurs; settings show a bounded Origin served reason, and only exact versioned manifest/segment plus Firefox evidence may pass the server gate.
cases.failure: DRM, hidden manifests, and unsupported MSE remain explicitly blocked or origin-served.

cases.setup: x-img, Monas, DASObjectStore, pairing, or network lookup times out.
cases.expected: The browser continues the original request within the bounded interception timeout.
cases.failure: No page error, retry loop, silent destination switch, credential disclosure, or browsing-history record.

cases.setup: A candidate request contains cookies/auth headers and a page contains hidden playlist links and background media.
cases.expected: The extension neither reads nor forwards credentials and does not open, traverse, crawl, or simulate navigation.
cases.failure: Any such path is rejected by static/privacy fixtures.
